# Definition of Done (DoD)

## Purpose

This document defines the minimum quality bar required before any work is considered complete in the CTOS Portfolio repository.

Applies to:

- Features
- Components
- Pages
- Documentation
- Refactors
- AI Agent Contributions
- Human Contributions

---

# Philosophy

Done does not mean:

- Code exists
- UI renders
- Build passes

Done means:

- Requirement satisfied
- Quality validated
- Experience validated
- Documentation updated
- Ready for production

---

# Source Of Truth

Before marking work complete, verify:

1. PRD Requirements
2. Roadmap Layer Requirements
3. AI Agent Operating System
4. Coding Standards
5. Architecture Decisions

---

# Universal Completion Criteria

Every task must satisfy:

- Requirement Complete
- Code Complete
- UX Complete
- Accessibility Complete
- Performance Complete
- Documentation Complete

---

# Feature Definition of Done

## Functional

- Feature works as expected
- Edge cases handled
- Empty states handled
- Error states handled

---

## Technical

- No console errors
- No hydration errors
- No TypeScript errors
- Build passes

---

## Experience

- UX validated
- Mobile validated
- Accessibility validated

---

# Component Definition of Done

Every component must:

- Have single responsibility
- Be reusable
- Be accessible
- Be documented
- Follow naming conventions

---

# Page Definition of Done

Every page must:

- Match roadmap intent
- Support primary user journey
- Contain required SEO metadata
- Be mobile friendly
- Be accessible

---

# Documentation Definition of Done

Documentation must:

- Be accurate
- Be updated
- Reflect implementation
- Avoid contradictions

---

# Accessibility Checklist

Required:

- Keyboard Navigation
- Focus States
- Semantic HTML
- Screen Reader Support
- Reduced Motion Support
- Contrast Compliance

---

# Performance Checklist

Targets:

- Lighthouse Performance >= 95
- Accessibility >= 100
- Best Practices >= 100
- SEO >= 100

---

# Motion Checklist

Every animation must:

- Have purpose
- Support understanding
- Respect reduced motion
- Maintain performance budget

---

# Authority Checklist

Verify:

- Evidence exists
- Claims are supported
- Projects are documented
- Trust signals are visible

---

# Content Checklist

Content must:

- Be concise
- Be professional
- Be evidence-based
- Avoid buzzwords

---

# Git Checklist

Before commit:

- Files organized
- Naming conventions followed
- No dead code
- No duplicate logic

---

# Pull Request Checklist

Every PR must include:

- Objective
- Scope
- Impact
- Validation Results

---

# Launch Readiness Checklist

Verify:

- Build Success
- Route Validation
- SEO Validation
- Accessibility Validation
- Performance Validation
- Analytics Validation

---

# AI Agent Completion Rules

Rule DOD-01

Never mark work complete without validation.

Rule DOD-02

Never skip accessibility checks.

Rule DOD-03

Never skip performance review.

Rule DOD-04

Documentation must be updated.

Rule DOD-05

Roadmap requirements must be satisfied.

---

# Rejection Criteria

Work is NOT DONE if:

- Requirement incomplete
- Accessibility missing
- Performance regression exists
- Documentation outdated
- UX degraded
- Technical debt introduced

---

# Final Definition of Done

A task is complete only when:

- Requirements satisfied
- Quality validated
- UX validated
- Accessibility validated
- Performance validated
- Documentation updated
- Production readiness confirmed

Only then may the task be considered DONE.
